description: vLLM v0.10–v0.15 operational metrics: KV cache pressure, TTFT/TPOT latency, token throughput, queue depth, and per-replica balance. Filter by namespace → model → pod.

panels.description: Requests currently being decoded across all selected replicas.
panels.title: Requests Running

panels.description: Requests in the scheduler queue waiting for a free slot. Growing queue = capacity pressure.
panels.title: Requests Waiting

panels.description: Requests preempted to CPU KV cache. Non-zero = GPU KV cache is under pressure.
panels.title: Requests Swapped

panels.description: Completed requests per second across selected model(s).
panels.title: Request Throughput

panels.description: Output tokens per second. Primary throughput signal for inference capacity planning.
panels.title: Token Generation Rate

panels.description: 99th percentile Time to First Token. Proxy for prefill latency. >1s impacts perceived responsiveness.
panels.title: TTFT P99

panels.description: GPU KV cache utilization (0–1). Above 0.9 = preemption risk. Values are per-engine; averaged across replicas here.
panels.title: GPU KV Cache Usage

panels.description: CPU KV cache utilization (0–1). Only meaningful when CPU offloading is enabled. Non-zero = GPU cache has been full at least once.
panels.title: CPU KV Cache Usage

panels.description: Fraction of prompt tokens served from prefix cache. High hit rate = cost savings on repeated prefixes. Requires v0.14+.
panels.title: Prefix Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: GPU KV cache utilization per pod over time. Pods consistently at >0.85 will experience preemptions and latency spikes.
panels.title: GPU KV Cache Usage Over Time

panels.description: Median Time to First Token — typical prefill latency
panels.title: TTFT P50

panels.description: 95th percentile TTFT — tail latency for prefill
panels.title: TTFT P95

panels.description: 99th percentile TTFT — worst-case prefill latency
panels.title: TTFT P99

panels.description: Median Time per Output Token — P50 decode speed. Lower is faster.
panels.title: TPOT P50

panels.description: 95th percentile TPOT — tail decode latency per token
panels.title: TPOT P95

panels.description: 99th percentile TPOT — worst-case decode speed per token
panels.title: TPOT P99

panels.description: TTFT percentiles over time. Spikes indicate compute-heavy prefill (long prompts, heavy load). Sustained elevation = need more GPU capacity or shorter context limits.
panels.title: Time to First Token Over Time

panels.description: Full request duration (queue wait + prefill + decode). P99 is the user-perceived worst case. Growing P99 with stable TTFT = decode bottleneck.
panels.title: End-to-End Request Duration Over Time

panels.description: TPOT percentiles over time. Rising TPOT = decode is slower, fewer concurrent batches possible. Correlate with requests_running.
panels.title: Time per Output Token Over Time

panels.description: Output tokens per second per model. Primary capacity metric — size GPU fleet to sustain this rate at target latency.
panels.title: Token Generation Rate

panels.description: Successful requests per second per model. Combine with token rate to understand avg response length trends.
panels.title: Request Throughput

panels.description: P50 / P95 prompt token count. Long prompts drive higher TTFT and VRAM usage. Growing P95 = context is getting longer over time.
panels.title: Prompt Length Distribution (tokens)

panels.description: P50 / P95 output token count. Drives TPOT and total GPU time per request. Growing P95 = users triggering longer generation.
panels.title: Output Length Distribution (tokens)

panels.description: Running (being decoded) / Waiting (queue) / Swapped (CPU offload) per model. Waiting growth = saturation. Swapped > 0 = KV cache full, preemptions imminent.
panels.title: Request Queue Depth Over Time

panels.description: KV cache preemptions per second. Any sustained preemption rate degrades latency — it means requests are being evicted from GPU cache and re-computed.
panels.title: Preemption Rate

panels.description: Active requests per replica. Even distribution = healthy load balancing. Skew = sticky sessions or readiness probe issues on some replicas.
panels.title: Running Requests per Pod

panels.description: Queue depth per replica. Any pod consistently non-zero while others are idle = load balancer not distributing evenly.
panels.title: Waiting Requests per Pod

title: vLLM Monitoring
